decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then reanimate them using spells like Animate Dead and Reanimate. The deck also utilizes cards like Faithless Looting and Lotus Petal to help set up the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disrupt their strategy to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a Death & Taxes strategy, utilizing disruptive creatures and removal spells to control the game. It leverages synergy between cards such as Stoneforge Mystic, Swords to Plowshares, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to disrupt the opponent's game plan while simultaneously applying pressure with efficient creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Recruiter of the Guard. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and versatility with the deck's ETB effects, giving it a strong board presence and resiliency against opposing threats.

The deck focuses on quickly filling your graveyard with powerful creature cards, then bringing them back onto the battlefield for a big explosive play. By combining cards like Unmask, Faithless Looting, and Collective Brutality to set up your graveyard, you can use cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave to bring back high-impact creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Griselbrand. The deck also includes various ways to disrupt your opponent's strategy, such as Cabal Therapy. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm your opponent with powerful reanimated creatures before they can react.

This deck focuses on early game aggression and tempo plays, using efficient spells and creatures to control the board and pressure the opponent. The key strategy is to utilize cheap spells like Lightning Bolt and Daze to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent to close out the game quickly. The deck also utilizes cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to find key cards and keep the pressure on the opponent. Additionally, cards like Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble help provide additional card advantage and deck manipulation.

The deck focuses on utilizing the card Doomsday to set up a specific sequence of cards in the library to win the game. It includes cards like Ponder and Brainstorm to dig for combo pieces, along with cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to generate mana quickly. Thassa's Oracle is included as part of the win condition, along with Lion's Eye Diamond for additional mana. The deck also includes disruption in the form of cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo and prevent the opponent from interfering with the game plan.

This combo deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard and then reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty to overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy is to use cards like Faithless Looting, Thoughtseize, and Unmask to get creatures into the graveyard, then use Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back to the battlefield. The deck also includes cards like Collective Brutality and Atraxa, Grand Unifier for added utility and disruption. With fast mana acceleration from cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual, this deck aims to win through explosive plays and quick reanimation of threats.

The deck focuses on using Show and Tell to quickly drop powerful creatures like Emrakul onto the battlefield, bypassing traditional mana costs. This combo strategy aims to overwhelm opponents with big threats early in the game, while using counterspells and card draw to protect the combo. By combining the potential of Sneak Attack and Show and Tell with powerful creatures and disruption, the deck aims to outpace and outplay opponents in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the board and disrupt the opponent with counter spells and land destruction while applying pressure with evasive creatures like Murktide Regent, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Delver of Secrets. By utilizing cards like Flow State, Mishra's Bauble, and Brainstorm, the deck aims to maintain card advantage and tempo advantage, allowing it to maintain a strong presence on the battlefield and quickly close out the game. The inclusion of cards like Lightning Bolt and Prismari Charm also provides additional flexibility and removal options.

The key concept of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. By controlling the board with cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Back to Basics, the deck aims to establish dominance and grind out victories in the late game. The inclusion of Murktide Regent provides a powerful threat that can close out games quickly, while cards like Ponder and Brainstorm help filter draws and find the right answers when needed.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and control strategies to establish a prison-like lock on the game. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and slow down their progress. The deck also includes powerful artifacts like Grim Monolith and Candelabra of Tawnos to generate mana and cast game-ending threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and dominate the board with powerful artifacts and disruptive effects.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with big creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, then using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to cheat them into play for a powerful combo finish. It uses cards like Unmask and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's plans, while also having ways to protect its own creatures with cards like Collective Brutality. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with huge threats early in the game, making it difficult for them to recover and control the board.

This deck focuses on creating a lock down situation through the use of artifacts, specifically artifacts that can control the opponent's actions and limit their options. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and prevent them from executing their strategy effectively. With powerful threats like Ugin, the Eye of the Storm and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, the deck can close out the game once it has established control. The key is to lock down the opponent while generating incremental advantage to eventually overwhelm them.

The main concept of this UWx Control deck is to control the game using a variety of efficient removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up card advantage and eventually winning the game with powerful finishers like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck's mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the spells in the deck and ensure consistency in casting its powerful spells.

This combo reanimator deck focuses on quickly discarding high-cost creatures like Griselbrand and Koma, and then bringing them back onto the battlefield with cards like Animate Dead and Reanimate. The deck utilizes Thoughtseize and Collective Brutality to disrupt the opponent's strategy while ramping up mana with Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal. Atraxa and Tinybones provide additional value and control, making it difficult for the opponent to stop the powerful combo plays. The deck is designed to overwhelm the opponent with big threats and efficient reanimation spells.

This deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing powerful creatures like Voice of Victory and Orcish Bowmasters to quickly defeat the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize to control the board state and remove threats. Additionally, it includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and damage output. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggressive strategy, with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent mana development.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly assemble the Cephalid Illusionist and Nomads en-Kor combo to mill through the deck, allowing for a quick Dread Return to bring back Thassa's Oracle for the win. The deck utilizes various spells and lands to protect the combo and manipulate the library. Additionally, Aether Vial and Urza's Saga provide additional support for the combo while providing flexibility and consistency. The deck aims to win the game as quickly and efficiently as possible through the combo interaction.
